title: Design Hash Table
description: Design a hash table with get(key), put(key, val), remove(key). Use chaining to handle collisions. Hash function: key % capacity.
key_concept: Hash function maps keys to buckets. Chaining: each bucket is a linked list of (key, value) pairs. Load factor determines resize threshold.

hints.L1: Use an array of lists (chaining). Hash: index = key % capacity. For each operation, find the bucket then linear scan for the key.
hints.L2: get: hash(key), scan bucket for key, return value or -1. put: hash, scan for existing key (update) or append new pair. remove: hash, find and delete pair.
hints.L3: Consider resizing when load factor (size/capacity) exceeds 0.5 — rehash all pairs into new larger array.
